overview.text: The Australian Broadcasting Corporation is Australia's national public broadcaster. ABC News operates 24/7 newsgathering across radio, television and digital. Talkgroups are typically used for news-crew coordination, outside-broadcast operations and major-event coverage.

items: ABC's Sydney headquarters is at Ultimo, with regional bureaux across NSW.
News crews coordinate with each other on production talkgroups during breaking events.

overview.text: The Australian Capital Territory Government provides State-and-local style government for the ACT, including ACT Emergency Services Agency (ACT Fire & Rescue, ACT Ambulance Service, ACT Rural Fire Service, ACT SES) and ACT Health. ACT services share PSN talkgroups for cross-border operations with NSW.

items: ACT Fire & Rescue, ACT Ambulance and ACT RFS share liaison talkgroups with NSW counterparts (especially around the Bombala/Cooma/Queanbeyan border).
ACT Policing is a function of the Australian Federal Police — ACT Policing-specific traffic appears under the AFP entry.

overview.text: Ausgrid owns and operates the electricity distribution network across Sydney, the Central Coast and the Hunter Region. It carries power from the high-voltage transmission grid to about 1.7 million homes and businesses.

items: Around 22,000 km² of NSW from Waterfall to Upper Hunter.
Roughly 30,000 km of overhead and underground network.
24/7 control room dispatches outage and emergency response crews.

overview.text: The Australian Federal Police is the federal police service of Australia. It enforces Commonwealth criminal law, protects Commonwealth interests including national security, and provides community policing in the Australian Capital Territory and external territories.

items: Established 1979 by the merger of the Commonwealth Police and the ACT Police.
Approximately 7,500 employees including more than 4,000 sworn officers.
Mission areas: counter-terrorism, organised crime and gangs, drugs, cybercrime, child protection operations, fraud and anti-corruption.
Provides Aviation Police at major airports including Sydney and Bankstown.
AFP officers in NSW work from the Sydney Office and the Aviation operations at Sydney Airport.
AFP coordinates with NSW Police on joint task forces (e.g. JOCG-style operations) using shared liaison talkgroups.

overview.text: The Australian Rail Track Corporation is a Commonwealth-owned company that manages and maintains most of the interstate standard-gauge rail network. In NSW it operates the Hunter Valley coal network and the interstate corridor, providing access to private operators.

items: Network Control Centres in Junee (NSW), Mile End (SA) and Broadmeadow (Hunter).
Inland Rail project — new Melbourne–Brisbane standard-gauge corridor.
Coordinates with NSW emergency services for level-crossing incidents and trespass.

overview.text: Blue Mountains City Council is the local government area covering the Blue Mountains region west of Sydney. Council services include parks and reserves, waste, roads, bushfire mitigation works and emergency operations support.

items: Population around 80,000 spread across 27 towns and villages.
World Heritage–listed Greater Blue Mountains Area covers most of the LGA.
Operates a 24/7 control centre during major incidents and runs an extensive volunteer bushfire-mitigation crew program alongside the RFS.

overview.text: Central Tablelands Water is a NSW county council that supplies bulk and reticulated drinking water to towns in Blayney, Cabonne and Weddin shires.

items: Constituent councils: Blayney, Cabonne, Weddin.
Sources from Lake Rowlands and treatment plant near Carcoar.
Serves towns including Grenfell, Canowindra, Molong, Cudal, Eugowra and Manildra.

overview.text: Corrective Services NSW is the agency responsible for adult prisons (correctional centres) and community-based supervision in NSW. It manages remand and sentenced inmates, escorts and prisoner transport, and the Community Corrections supervised-orders system.

items: Operates around 36 correctional centres including maximum, medium and minimum-security facilities.
Inmate population in the high 12,000s with thousands more on community orders.
Specialist units include the Security and Investigations Branch, the Corrective Services Industries (work programs) and Community Corrections.

overview.text: Endeavour Energy operates the electricity distribution network across Sydney's Greater West, the Blue Mountains, the Southern Highlands and the Illawarra — connecting around 2.7 million people and a million end-customers.

items: Network area roughly 25,000 km².
Around 38,000 km of distribution lines.
24/7 control centre dispatches network-emergency crews and coordinates with NSW emergency services for downed-wire incidents.

overview.text: Fire and Rescue NSW (FRNSW) is the primary fire and emergency service for urban areas in New South Wales, responsible for protecting life, property and the environment in cities, larger towns and key industrial areas.

items: Established in 1884 (formerly NSW Fire Brigades).
Operates approximately 335 fire stations across NSW.
Responds to more than 130,000 incidents each year.

sections.title: ⚠ Priority Codes
sections.intro: Quick reference for Fire and Rescue NSW radio priorities.

rows: Serious incidents, persons trapped, major fire escalation, firefighter in danger.
Crew finds a job while driving — medical, accident, or fire not yet reported.
Tactical updates, fire conditions, strategy changes, resource changes.
Final summary of actions taken and outcome.
Routine, non-incident traffic (station moves, returning to area, etc.).

sections.title: 📟 Status Codes
sections.intro: Status codes as used by appliances when reporting to FireCom.

sections.title: 🧭 Regional Zones
sections.intro: Fire and Rescue NSW Regional zones grouped by direction.

rows: Bellingen, Bowraville, Coffs Harbour, Dorrigo, Forster, Gloucester, Kempsey, Laurieton, Macksville, Nambucca Heads, Port Macquarie, Sawtell, South West Rocks, Taree, Tea Gardens, Urunga, Wauchope, Wingham, Woolgoolga

Alstonville, Ballina, Bangalow, Brunswick Heads, Byron Bay, Casino, Coraki, Evans Head, Goonellabah, Grafton, Kingscliff, Kyogle, Lismore, Maclean, Mullumbimby, Murwillumbah, South Grafton, Tweed Heads, Banora Point, Yamba
Armidale, Barraba, Bingara, Boggabri, Coonabarabran, Glen Innes, Gunnedah, Guyra, Inverell, Manilla, Moree, Narrabri, Quirindi, Tamworth, Tenterfield, Uralla, Walcha, Warialda, Wee Waa, Werris Creek
Batemans Bay, Bega, Berry, Bombala, Braidwood, Cooma, Eden, Jindabyne, Merimbula, Moruya, Narooma, Nowra, Perisher Valley, Queanbeyan, Thredbo, Ulladulla, Shoalhaven
Batlow, Boorowa, Bowral, Bundanoon, Cootamundra, Cowra, Crookwell, Goulburn, Grenfell, Gundagai, Harden, Mittagong, Moss Vale, Tumut, Yass, Young
Albury Central, Albury Civic, North Albury, Culcairn, Deniliquin, Finley, Henty, Holbrook, Jerilderie, Lockhart, Moama, Mulwala, Tocumwal, Tumbarumba, Turvey Park, Wagga Wagga
Bourke, Brewarrina, Cobar, Coolah, Coonamble, Gilgandra, Lightning Ridge, Narromine, Nyngan, Parkes, Peak Hill, Trangie, Walgett, Warren, Wellington
Aberdeen, Bathurst, Blayney, Canowindra, Denman, Gulgong, Kandos, Kelso, Merriwa, Molong, Mudgee, Murrurundi, Muswellbrook, Oberon, Orange, Scone, Singleton
Balranald, Broken Hill, Broken Hill South, Condobolin, Coolamon, Griffith, Hay, Hillston, Junee, Lake Cargelligo, Leeton, Narrandera, Temora, Wentworth, West Wyalong, Yenda

sections.title: 🏙 Metropolitan Zones
sections.intro: Fire and Rescue NSW Metropolitan zones grouped by direction.

rows: Belmont, Cardiff, Carrington, Charlestown, Jesmond, Lambton, Mayfield West, Merewether, Minmi, Morisset, Newcastle, Redhead, Salamander Bay, Stockton, Swansea, Tarro, Teralba, Tingira Heights, Toronto, Wallsend, Wangi Wangi, Holmesville
Bateau Bay, Berkeley Vale, Budgewoi, Doyalson, Gosford, Hamlyn Terrace, Kariong, Ourimbah, Saratoga, Terrigal, The Entrance, Toukley, Umina, Wyoming, Wyong, Kincumber
Abermain, Bellbird, Branxton / Greta, Cessnock, Dungog, East Maitland, Kearsley, Kurri Kurri, Maitland, Morpeth, Paxton, Raymond Terrace, Rutherford, Salamander Bay, Singleton, Heddon Greta
Albion Park, Balgownie, Bulli, Coledale, Dapto, Helensburgh, Kiama, Shellharbour, Thirroul, Unanderra, Warrawong, Wollongong
Arncliffe, Botany, Bundeena, Cronulla, Engadine, Hurstville, Kogarah, Maroubra, Mascot, Matraville, Menai, Miranda, Mortdale, Randwick, Riverwood, Sutherland, Port Botany / Kurnell
Busby, Camden, Campbelltown, Horningsea Park, Ingleburn, Liverpool, Macquarie Fields, Narellan, Picton, Rosemeadow, St Andrews, Oran Park, Narellan Vale
Alexandria, Bondi, City of Sydney, Darlington, Glebe, Pyrmont, Redfern, MLC Centre (City of Sydney East), Woollahra, City of Sydney East
Avalon Beach, Beecroft, Berowra, Crows Nest, Dee Why, Forestville, Gordon, Hornsby, Lane Cove, Manly, Mona Vale, Mosman, Narrabeen, Neutral Bay
Ashfield, Balmain, Bankstown, Burwood, Campsie, Chester Hill, Concord, Drummoyne, Lakemba, Leichhardt, Marrickville, Newtown, Revesby, Rhodes area
Blacktown, Castle Hill, Cranebrook / Ropes Crossing, Huntingwood, Mount Druitt, Penrith, Regentville, Riverstone
Bonnyrigg Heights, Cabramatta, Eastwood, Fairfield, Wentworthville, Silverwater, Smithfield, Rydalmere
Blackheath, Glenbrook, Katoomba, Lawson, Mount Victoria, Richmond, Springwood, Wallerawang, Wentworth Falls, Windsor

sections.title: 🚛 Appliance Prefixes
sections.intro: Common Fire and Rescue NSW appliance prefixes, grouped by type.

rows: Pumper fitted with Compressed Air Foam System
Tanker with Compressed Air Foam System
Tanker with rescue tools and equipment
Moving crews, rehab at major incidents

overview.text: Forestry Corporation of NSW manages around 2 million hectares of State Forests for timber production, recreation, fire management, biodiversity and Aboriginal cultural heritage. It maintains an internal fire-fighting capability and works closely with the RFS during the bushfire season.

items: Plantation and native-forest harvesting; timber sales for hardwood and softwood products.
Operational fire-management capability with crews, dozers, water-bombing aircraft contracts.
Recreation: 100+ campgrounds, 4WD trails, mountain-bike parks (e.g. Penrose, Cumberland, Sugar Pines).

overview.text: Hatzolah Medical Sydney is a volunteer first-response EMS organisation serving Sydney's eastern suburbs. Members are equipped with medical bags and respond by car or on foot to medical emergencies in their community, working in cooperation with NSW Ambulance.

items: Operates predominantly in the Bondi, Bondi Junction and Eastern Suburbs Jewish community area.
First responders only — patient transport is performed by NSW Ambulance.
Crews carry portable PSN radios for liaison with NSWAS during incidents.

overview.text: Hunter Water provides drinking water, wastewater and recycled-water services to around 600,000 people across the Lower Hunter — Newcastle, Lake Macquarie, Maitland, Cessnock, Port Stephens and Dungog.

items: Around 250,000 connected properties.
Major sources: Chichester Dam, Grahamstown Dam, Tomago/Anna Bay sandbeds.
24/7 incident response for main breaks and sewage overflows.

overview.text: Marine Rescue NSW is the State's primary volunteer marine search-and-rescue service. It coordinates on-water rescue, vessel safety monitoring (logged Trip Watch), missing-vessel response and emergency communications across the NSW coast and major inland waterways.

items: Established in 2009 by merging the Australian Volunteer Coast Guard, Royal Volunteer Coastal Patrol and Volunteer Rescue Association marine units.
Around 3,000 volunteers across 46 units from Point Danger to Eden, plus Alpine Lakes and Moama on the Murray.
Operates a fleet of more than 100 rescue vessels and a 24/7 State Communications Centre.
Tasked through Marine Area Command (NSW Police) for life-at-risk incidents.

overview.text: NETS is a specialist 24/7 emergency-transport service for critically unwell newborns, infants and children in NSW and the ACT. NETS teams (clinician + nurse) move patients between hospitals by road, helicopter or fixed-wing aircraft.

items: Single clinical phone number used by referring hospitals to activate NETS.
NETS coordinates transfers between paediatric tertiary centres (Sydney Children's, Westmead, John Hunter Children's).
Teams use NSW Ambulance and aeromedical assets for movement, with NETS-specific clinical talkgroups for handover.

overview.text: NSW Ambulance provides emergency medical response, aeromedical retrieval, rescue operations and non-emergency transport across more than 800,000 km² of New South Wales. Over 1.2 million incidents are handled yearly.

items: Serves 7.9+ million people across metro, regional and remote NSW.
250+ ambulance stations and response locations.
Road fleet travels ~40–45 million km per year.
One of the largest ambulance fleets in the world (around 1,500 vehicles).
{b:Clinical Operations:} front-line road paramedics and specialist roles.
{b:Aeromedical Operations:} helicopters & fixed-wing retrieval teams.
{b:Special Operations Team (SOT) / RAA:} remote & complex rescue environments.
{b:Critical Care:} CCPs, flight nurses & retrieval doctors.
Ambulance Control Centres in Sydney, Newcastle, Wollongong and Dubbo.
Aeromedical Control Centre for helicopter and fixed-wing dispatch.
CAD / MDT and GPS-based nearest resource assignment.

sections.title: 📟 Category Codes
sections.intro: NSW Ambulance category codes and whether they usually run lights and sirens.

rows: Patient is not breathing, unconscious, or in a serious time-critical condition.
Patient is conscious and breathing but still serious and requires urgent response.
Moderate condition requiring ambulance response but not lights & sirens.
Notify hospital – doctor required on arrival
Arriving at hospital with DOA for certification
Live wires down – electrical authority required

sections.title: 🎧 Common Radio Phrases
sections.intro: Common terms you will hear on NSW Ambulance talkgroups.

rows: On meal break; not normally available unless escalated.

sections.title: 📡 Talkgroups & Monitoring Notes
items: Each NSW Ambulance region has one or more {b:primary dispatch} talkgroups.
{b:Aeromedical} uses dedicated or shared retrieval channels.
Major incidents may link FRNSW, RFS, SES, Police on shared or patched channels.
Some talkgroups may be encrypted or carry limited traffic.
Traffic volume increases during storms, heatwaves and major events.
Do not publish identifiable patient information.

overview.text: Aircraft registrations for NSW Ambulance, Police, Westpac, Surf Life Saving, RFDS and military assets operating in or near NSW. Use the Agencies search in the sidebar to jump to a specific registration.

sections.title: 🛩 Air Ambulance NSW – Fixed Wing
sections.intro: NSW Ambulance operates a fleet of fixed-wing aircraft for urgent patient transfers and long-distance retrievals.

sections.title: 🚁 NSW Ambulance / Toll HEMS
sections.intro: AW139 helicopters operated by Toll Helicopters on behalf of NSW Ambulance.

sections.title: 🚁 Westpac Rescue Helicopters
sections.intro: Operated by Westpac on behalf of agencies including NSW Ambulance and Tasmania Police.

sections.title: 🚔 Police Aviation
sections.intro: Police aviation assets from NSW and Victoria.

sections.title: 🚑 Interstate Ambulance
sections.intro: Ambulance assets from Victoria and South Australia.

sections.title: 🌙 Royal Flying Doctor Service
sections.intro: Fixed-wing aircraft used by RFDS sections across Australia.

overview.text: NSW DPI delivers programs covering agriculture, biosecurity, fisheries, forestry research, animal welfare, and emergency management for primary industries. It is now part of the NSW Department of Primary Industries and Regional Development (DPIRD).

items: Biosecurity and animal-disease response (e.g. fruit fly, varroa mite, lumpy skin disease preparedness).
Fisheries compliance — DPI Fisheries Officers regulate recreational and commercial fishing.
Plant health, weed management and biosecurity emergency response.
Land-management programs and rural-resilience support.

overview.text: The NSW Environment Protection Authority is the State's primary environmental regulator. It licenses and audits polluting industries, responds to pollution incidents, prosecutes environmental offences, and regulates radiation, chemicals, contaminated land and waste.

items: 24/7 Environment Line for pollution reports.
Hazmat support to fire and emergency services for chemical and radiation incidents.
Environmental forensics and authorised-officer enforcement powers.

overview.text: NSW Mines Rescue is a service of Coal Services Pty Ltd (jointly owned by the NSW black-coal industry and the CFMEU). It provides specialist underground rescue capability, mine-emergency response and breathing-apparatus expertise to NSW coal mines and metalliferous operations.

items: Stations at Argenton (Hunter), Singleton, Lithgow, Woonona and Wollongong.
On-call brigade members are also working miners at member sites.
Capabilities: long-duration BA, irrespirable-atmosphere rescue, gas analysis, sealing teams, casualty extraction.

overview.text: The NSW National Parks and Wildlife Service manages more than 870 national parks, nature reserves and historic sites covering around 7.5 million hectares — about 9% of NSW. NPWS is a major fire-fighting agency and the lead manager for park-based emergencies and search-and-rescue.

items: Field staff trained as remote-area firefighters and as Park Rangers.
Hazard-reduction burning and fire suppression alongside RFS and Forestry Corporation.
Pest, weed and threatened-species management programs.
Coordinates with NSW Police Rescue and SES for park search and rescue.

overview.text: The New South Wales Police Force is the primary law-enforcement agency for NSW. It is one of the largest police organisations in the English-speaking world, providing 24/7 emergency response, criminal investigation, traffic policing, public-order management and specialist operations across the state.

items: Established in 1862 as the unified NSW Police Force.
Around 17,000 sworn officers and 4,000 administrative staff.
Operates from roughly 470 stations grouped into Police Area Commands (metro) and Police Districts (regional).
Specialist units include PolAir, Marine Area Command, Counter Terrorism, Dog Unit, Rescue & Bomb Disposal, Tactical Operations Unit, Negotiators, Public Order & Riot Squad.
Routine NSWPF radio traffic on the PSN is heavily encrypted; many talkgroups carry minimal cleartext.
Some specialist and tasking channels remain in clear or partially in clear.
Be aware that publishing anything that could compromise an active operation or identify victims/officers is illegal under NSW law.

overview.text: The NSW Rural Fire Service (NSW RFS) is the world's largest volunteer fire service and the lead combat agency for bush and grass fires in New South Wales. It protects communities, property and the environment across around 95% of the state.

sections.title: ⚠ Priority Codes
sections.intro: RFS radio priority levels used when calling Fire Control.

rows: Goulburn Mulwaree, Upper Lachlan, Yass Valley
Bourke, Brewarrina, Central Darling, Cobar, Unincorporated

sections.title: 🚛 Appliance Categories
sections.intro: NSW RFS appliance categories (CAT types) commonly heard on radio and seen in CAD / logs.

rows: 4x4 or 6x6 crew / single cab (over 4000 litre) tanker.

overview.text: The NSW State Emergency Service is the lead combat agency for floods, storms and tsunamis in NSW. It also provides general rescue, vertical/cliff rescue, and is the primary land-based response for severe-weather incidents.

items: Established in 1955 as the State Emergency Service.
Around 10,000 volunteer members across 220+ units.
Lead agency for storm, flood and tsunami response under the State Emergency and Rescue Management Act.
Capabilities include flood rescue (in-water and boat), road-crash rescue, vertical rescue, urban search and rescue, and community education.
Activations escalate from local Unit through Region to State Operations Centre at Wollongong.
Major events frequently patch SES with FRNSW, RFS, NSW Police and NSW Ambulance on shared liaison talkgroups.

overview.text: The NSW Telco Authority builds, maintains and operates the State's Public Safety Network (PSN) — the digital trunked radio network that consolidates the previous Government Radio Network (GRN) and serves the police, fire, ambulance, SES, health, transport, utilities and many councils.

items: TGA owns the network — its own talkgroups are typically site-test, engineering and field-maintenance traffic.
Operates a 24/7 Network Operations Centre.
Migration of NSW agencies onto the PSN consolidates emergency communications statewide.

office-of-the-sheriff-of-nsw.title: ⚖ Office of the Sheriff of NSW

badges.label: NSW Department of Communities and Justice

overview.text: The Sheriff's Office of NSW enforces civil orders of NSW courts, provides court security and prisoner escort, and conducts jury management. Sheriff's Officers are sworn personnel with limited authorised powers.

items: Execution of writs and warrants (e.g. property repossession, debt enforcement).
Court-room and court-house security.
Jury empanelment and management.
In-custody escort within court precincts.

overview.text: Property NSW is the NSW Government's property-management entity. It manages a large portfolio of owned and leased commercial buildings, oversees major precincts (e.g. Darling Harbour, The Rocks, Sydney Olympic Park as Place Management NSW) and provides facilities-management services to other agencies.

items: Owned office portfolio in Sydney CBD and major regional centres.
Heritage and place-management at iconic precincts.
Security and operations talkgroups support 24/7 site management.

overview.text: Rental Groups are PSN talkgroups leased on a short or long-term basis to organisations that don't have their own permanent allocation. They cover event production, contractor crews, pop-up operations and one-off government projects.

items: Major-event security and traffic (e.g. Vivid Sydney, NYE, Mardi Gras, Sydney Royal Easter Show, marathon and triathlon support).
Construction tier-one contractors during major-project blockades.
Short-term agency surge capacity.
Renter identity is rarely advertised in the PSN talkgroup name and frequently changes.
Encryption settings vary by hire customer.

overview.text: The Royal Society for the Prevention of Cruelty to Animals (NSW) is an independent non-government charity. RSPCA Inspectors are authorised officers under the Prevention of Cruelty to Animals Act and investigate cruelty complaints, seize animals at risk, and prosecute offences.

items: Operates animal shelters, veterinary hospitals and an inspectorate.
Cruelty hotline (1300 CRUELTY) takes around 16,000+ reports per year in NSW.
Inspectors carry powers of entry and seizure but are not police; serious cases are escalated to NSW Police.

overview.text: Shared Liaison Groups are talkgroups that bring multiple emergency services onto a single common channel for incident coordination. They are used at major incidents where two or more services need to talk to each other in real time without leaving the PSN.

items: FRNSW + NSW Ambulance + NSW Police at structure fires and rescues.
RFS + FRNSW + Forestry + NPWS during major bushfires.
SES + NSW Ambulance + NSW Police during flood operations.
Multi-agency event management for major events (NYE, Vivid, Royal Show).

overview.text: Special Constables are appointed under the Police Act 1990 with specified, limited police powers — typically restricted to a particular employer, premises, or function (e.g. NSW Parliament, court precincts, Reserve Bank, certain Commonwealth sites).

items: Not full sworn members of the NSW Police Force.
Appointed for protective-security duties at specific establishments.
Coordinated with NSW Police on shared liaison channels for incidents on covered premises.

overview.text: St John Ambulance NSW is a volunteer first-aid charity. In NSW, primary emergency ambulance services are provided by NSW Ambulance — St John's role is community first-aid, event medical coverage, training and youth (Cadet) programs.

items: Different from St John Ambulance WA / NT — those subdivisions are the primary EMS in those jurisdictions.
In NSW, St John volunteers are commonly seen at sporting events, festivals and shows providing on-site first aid.
Coordination with NSW Ambulance occurs on event-specific liaison talkgroups.

overview.text: Surf Life Saving NSW is the peak body for volunteer surf lifesaving and beach rescue across NSW. It coordinates beach patrols, swift-water rescue on the coast, jet-ski rescue, helicopter rescue and aerial surveillance, and community drowning-prevention programs.

items: Around 78,000 members across 129 surf lifesaving clubs in 11 branches.
Operates the Westpac Lifesaver Rescue Helicopter Service jointly with NSW Ambulance.
Provides Australian Lifeguard Service (paid lifeguards) on more than 60 beaches outside volunteer patrol hours.
Rescue Water Craft (jet ski) operators stage at high-risk locations during summer.

overview.text: Sutherland Shire Council is the local government for southern Sydney, covering an area from Caringbah and Cronulla in the east through to Engadine, Menai and Royal National Park to the south and west.

items: Population around 230,000.
Operates lifeguards, parks, waste, environmental health, emergency-management coordination.
Talkgroups support council operations including ranger patrols, parks, libraries and waste services.

overview.text: The Sydney Opera House is one of Australia's most recognised landmarks and a UNESCO World Heritage site. The Opera House Trust manages venue operations, security, fire-safety, technical production and patron services across multiple performance halls.

items: Hosts more than 1,800 performances per year drawing 1.4M attendees.
Operates a 24/7 site security and operations team — talkgroups support venue, FOH, stage door, security and maintenance.
Coordinates with NSW Police and FRNSW for events and emergencies on-site.

overview.text: Sydney Water provides drinking water, wastewater, recycled water and some stormwater services to about 5.4 million people in Greater Sydney, the Illawarra and the Blue Mountains. Its network is the largest urban water network in Australia.

items: Around 22,000 km of water mains and 26,000 km of sewer mains.
Treatment plants include Prospect (water) and several large coastal wastewater plants.
24/7 incident response for main breaks, sewage overflows and chemical spills.

overview.text: Transport for NSW is the lead State agency for the planning, regulation, integration and delivery of transport services and infrastructure across NSW. It oversees buses, ferries, light rail, trains, roads, maritime safety and active transport.

items: Established 2011, expanded in 2019 to absorb Roads & Maritime Services (RMS).
Operating divisions cover Sydney Trains, NSW TrainLink, Sydney Metro, Sydney Ferries, regional services and Transport Management Centres.
Operates the Transport Management Centre at Eveleigh — coordinates real-time response across road, rail, bus, ferry and light-rail.

overview.text: Sydney Ferries operates passenger ferry services across Sydney Harbour and the Parramatta River under contract to Transport for NSW. The fleet includes the iconic Manly Freshwater-class, the Emerald-class commuter ferries, RiverCats and Lady-class vessels.

items: F1 Manly · F2 Taronga Zoo · F3 Parramatta River · F4 Pyrmont Bay · F5 Neutral Bay · F6 Mosman Bay · F7 Eastern Suburbs · F8 Cockatoo Island · F9 Watsons Bay · F10 Blackwattle Bay.
Major wharves: Circular Quay, Barangaroo, Manly, Parramatta, Taronga Zoo.

overview.text: NSW Maritime is the regulator of recreational and commercial vessel safety on NSW waterways. It is part of Transport for NSW and works closely with the Marine Area Command (NSW Police), Marine Rescue NSW and AMSA.

items: Boating-safety regulation, licensing and registration.
Pollution response and aids-to-navigation maintenance.
Boating Safety Officers patrol popular waterways during peak periods.

badges.label: Sydney Trains / NSW TrainLink / Sydney Metro

overview.text: Heavy rail in NSW is delivered by three TfNSW operating entities: Sydney Trains (suburban), NSW TrainLink (intercity, regional and XPT services) and Sydney Metro (driverless metro). The network spans more than 1,500 km of track and 300+ stations.

items: Sydney Trains — eight suburban lines (T1–T9) plus the Airport line.
NSW TrainLink — Blue Mountains, Central Coast & Newcastle, South Coast, Southern Highlands, Hunter, plus regional XPT and Xplorer services.
Sydney Metro — Metro North West & City line; under expansion via Metro West and Western Sydney Airport line.
Rail Operations Centre coordinates response across the heavy-rail network with NSW Police Transit & Public Safety Command.

overview.text: TfNSW Roads (formerly Roads and Maritime Services) manages the State road network — motorways, highways, regional roads and major bridges and tunnels. It runs the Transport Management Centre, dispatches the Traffic Emergency Patrol, and oversees the road-asset network.

items: Transport Management Centre (TMC) — 24/7 incident coordination across road, rail, bus, ferry and light-rail.
Traffic Emergency Patrol — clears stalled vehicles and minor incidents to restore flow on motorways.
Heavy-vehicle and over-dimensional permit operations.

overview.text: Unconfirmed Groups are PSN talkgroups whose user agency has not been positively identified by the monitoring community, or where the prior allocation has changed. The TGIDs listed here may be active, dormant, reassigned, or temporarily allocated for testing.

items: If you have first-hand information about any of these talkgroups (especially their owning agency or function), please share via the project's data sources page so they can be re-categorised.
Verification typically requires distinct activity patterns, alias hints in audio, and corroboration with public-record information.
Never publish identifying information about private individuals heard on these channels.

overview.text: VRA Rescue NSW is a volunteer general land-rescue service accredited by the State Rescue Board. Squads provide road-crash rescue, vertical rescue, swift-water and flood rescue, and general assistance to NSW Police, NSW Ambulance and other emergency services.

items: Around 30 squads spread across regional NSW.
Primary or secondary RCR (Road Crash Rescue) authority in many regional areas where there is no FRNSW or career RFS rescue capability.
Tasked through NSW Police VKG and dispatched with paramedics and fire on shared incident talkgroups.

overview.text: WaterNSW operates the State's bulk water-storage network — including Warragamba, the Snowy water transfers, the Hunter and the Murray-Darling regulated rivers. It supplies bulk water to Sydney Water and to regional councils, and licenses water for irrigation and town supply.

items: Operates more than 40 large dams including Warragamba, Burrendong, Wyangala, Copeton and Glenbawn.
Manages the State's water-licence and metering system.
Coordinates flood operations of regulated dams during major events.

overview.text: The Western Sydney Parklands Trust manages a 5,280-hectare urban parkland — one of the world's largest. It runs across Liverpool, Fairfield and Blacktown, providing recreation areas, cycleways, leasehold farms, and large bushland reserves.

items: Established 2006 by Act of Parliament.
Over 60 km of trails, picnic areas, dog parks and adventure playgrounds.
Ranger and operations talkgroups support 7-day-a-week site management.

overview.text: Youth Justice NSW (formerly Juvenile Justice) supervises young people aged 10–17 in detention and on community-based orders. It operates Youth Justice Centres, runs court-ordered supervision and counselling programs, and provides bail-supervision and post-release support.

items: Operates a small number of Youth Justice Centres across NSW.
Community Youth Justice teams supervise young people on bail and community orders.
Specialist programs include the Joint Support Program for high-needs detainees.
